{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period burial mounds distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest burial mound at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period burial mounds distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest burial mound at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period burial mounds distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest burial mound at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period burial mounds distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest burial mound at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Otomezuka Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Otomezuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Otomezuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後方墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period burial mounds distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest burial mound at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period burial mounds distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest burial mound at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period burial mounds distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest burial mound at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period burial mounds distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest burial mound at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period burial mounds distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest burial mound at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period burial mounds distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest burial mound at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period burial mounds distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest burial mound at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period Kofuns distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest Kofun at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period Kofuns distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest Kofun at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period Kofuns distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest Kofun at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period Kofuns distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest Kofun at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period Kofuns distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest Kofun at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period Kofuns distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest Kofun at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period Kofuns distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest Kofun at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is believed to be the oldest Kofun at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period Kofuns distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest Kofun at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period Kofuns distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest Kofun at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period Kofuns distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest Kofun at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name =Otomezuka Kofun | native_name =処女塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Otomezuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Otomezuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E|region:JP-28|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|40.92|N|135|14|50.59|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Otomezuka Kofun'''|処女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Mikagezuka neighborhood of [[Higashinada-ku, Kobe]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191282|title= 処女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Otomezuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an [[alluvial fan]] formed by the Ishiyagawa River, and is one of the several early Kofun period Kofuns distributed on the coastal plain at the southern foot of [[Mount Rokkō]]. The total length of the tumulus, which had ''[[fukiishi]]'' its surface, is estimated to be about 70 meters after the restoration. It is believed to be the oldest Kofun at the southern foot of Mount Rokkō. The 100-meter {{nihongo|[[Nishimotomezuka Kofun]]|西求女塚古墳||}} is about 2,000 meters west of this location, and the 80-meter {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} is about 1,500 meters to the east. From this positional arrangement, the Otomezuka Kofun has long been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''[[Man'yōshū]]'' and the ''[[Yamato Monogatari]]'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Since 1979, the tumulus has been restored and maintained as a historic park. The tumulus is about a three-minute walk from [[Ishiyagawa Station]] on the [[Hanshin Electric Railway]] [[Hanshin Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]
